In short, you've got to determine your NOI, your net operating income. Net operating income is determined by your income, gross collected income, less all expenses before any CAPX. What's CAPX? That's major non-recurring expenses on the property, like a roof or a clubhouse or floors, maybe throughout the public parts of the building that you're not going to do over and over again. Every business runs off of its NOI, your business, your apartment deal. The value is determined by NOI. The debt that it can handle is determined by the NOI. This is probably the second most important number to me when evaluating a deal. The NOI determines the true value of your business, the net operating income. That's after expenses. Before debt, number determines the true value of your business and why it's so important to understand. Banks and lenders you approach for financing will not care about your credit or the appraisal on the property. They're going to care first about the NOI.
